description: The SigningBackend trait contract (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1411) specifies 'Return the public key as DER-encoded SubjectPublicKeyInfo'. Every other signing backend (aws-kms, local-signing, pkcs11) returns actual SPKI DER bytes. The tpm2 backend instead marshals the TPM2B_PUBLIC structure (line 365) and returns that. The comment on line 363-364 even acknowledges this: 'Marshal the TPM2B_PUBLIC structure. Callers can parse the algorithm-specific fields from the marshalled bytes.' This violates the trait contract. Callers (e.g. secretx-signature) that pass the output to from_public_key_der() will fail. Fix: extract the raw key material from the TPM2B_PUBLIC and construct a proper SPKI DER encoding using the spki or p256/rsa crates.
title: tpm2 public_key_der returns TPM2B_PUBLIC, not SPKI DER
